extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL attacked with 1km to go after ROGLIČ refused to share pace, won by 2 seconds but lost GC by bonus seconds arithmetic.
tactical_patterns: Soudal Quick-Step over-paced earlier climbs with BERNAL, exhausting domestiques before finale—risky investment for marginal gains.
EVENEPOEL repeatedly demanded turns from ROGLIČ while ahead, signaling psychological warfare before final kilometer attack.
SOLER attacked without protecting ALMEIDA's GC position, revealing UAE's internal hierarchy confusion on selective mountain stages.
KARAPAZ resisted multiple break attacks on penultimate climb, showing champion's resilience despite ultimately being caught 9km out.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: dominant power display forcing ROGLIČ into passive tactics, but celebration cost 2 seconds and potential GC lead
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: tactically astute refusal to work preserved 4-second bonus gap, retaining GC despite weaker finale
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: suffered badly on successive climbs despite team support, recovery timeline appears significantly longer than anticipated
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: neutral: strong enough to attack late but unclear team role suggests UAE lacks cohesive GC protection plan
rider_signals.KARAPAZ Richard: positive: survived break attacks until 9km remaining, showing capacity to defend advantage despite not winning
rider_signals.VAN GILS Maxim: positive: last breakaway rider caught confirms ability to sustain efforts on progressive altitude stages
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: well-placed despite SOLER's unsupported attack, suggesting personal form sufficient without team commitment
